content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers.

Find the number of subsequences SSS having length MMM such that:


MMM >\gt> 111;
For any pair of indices (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) in the subsequence SSS, (lcm(M,Si)‚ąíSi)=(lcm(M,Sj)‚ąíSj)(lcm(M, S_i) - S_i) = (lcm(M, S_j) - S_j)(lcm(M,Si‚Äč)‚ąíSi‚Äč)=(lcm(M,Sj‚Äč)‚ąíSj‚Äč). Here lcm(a,b)lcm(a,b)lcm(a,b) denotes the lcm of integers aaa and bbb.
Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note that, a subsequence of an array is obtained by deleting the elements at some (possibly none or all) indices of the array. For example, all the subsequences of array [1,2,2][1, 2, 2][1,2,2] are {[A1],[A2],[A3],[A1,A2],[A1,A3],[A2,A3],[A1,A2,A3]}\{ [A_1], [A_2], [A_3], [A_1, A_2], [A_1, A_3], [A_2, A_3], [A_1, A_2, A_3] \}{[A1‚Äč],[A2‚Äč],[A3‚Äč],[A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč],[A1‚Äč,A3‚Äč],[A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč],[A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč]}, that is, {[1],[2],[2],[1,2],[1,2],[2,2],[1,2,2]}\{ [1], [2], [2], [1, 2], [1, 2], [2, 2], [1, 2, 2] \}{[1],[2],[2],[1,2],[1,2],[2,2],[1,2,2]}.


Input Format

The first line of input contains TTT, the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of elements in the array.
The next line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Sum of NNN of over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is no subsequence satisfying all the conditions.


Test case 222: The valid subsequences are: {A1,A2},{A1,A3},{A2,A3},\{A_1, A_2\}, \{A_1, A_3\}, \{A_2, A_3\},{A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč},{A1‚Äč,A3‚Äč},{A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč}, and {A1,A2,A3}.\{A_1, A_2, A_3\}.{A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč}.
For subsequence {A1,A3}\{A_1, A_3\}{A1‚Äč,A3‚Äč}, (lcm(2,A1)‚ąíA1)=(lcm(2,A3)‚ąíA3)=0(lcm(2, A_1) - A_1) = (lcm(2, A_3) - A_3) = 0(lcm(2,A1‚Äč)‚ąíA1‚Äč)=(lcm(2,A3‚Äč)‚ąíA3‚Äč)=0.
Similarly, for all pairs in the subsequence {A1,A2,A3}\{A_1, A_2, A_3\}{A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč}, (lcm(3,Si)‚ąíSi)=(lcm(3,Sj)‚ąíSj)=4(lcm(3, S_i) -S_i) = (lcm(3, S_j) - S_j) = 4(lcm(3,Si‚Äč)‚ąíSi‚Äč)=(lcm(3,Sj‚Äč)‚ąíSj‚Äč)=4.


Test case 333: The valid subsequences are: {A1,A2},{A3,A4},{A4,A5},{A3,A5},\{A_1, A_2\}, \{A_3, A_4\}, \{A_4, A_5\}, \{A_3, A_5\},{A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč},{A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč},{A4‚Äč,A5‚Äč},{A3‚Äč,A5‚Äč}, and {A3,A4,A5}.\{A_3, A_4, A_5\}.{A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč,A5‚Äč}.
For subsequence {A1,A2}\{A_1, A_2\}{A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč}, (lcm(2,A1)‚ąíA1)=(lcm(2,A2)‚ąíA2)(lcm(2, A_1) - A_1) = (lcm(2, A_2) - A_2)(lcm(2,A1‚Äč)‚ąíA1‚Äč)=(lcm(2,A2‚Äč)‚ąíA2‚Äč).  Same condition holds for subsequences {A3,A4},{A4,A5},{A3,A5}\{A_3, A_4\}, \{A_4, A_5\}, \{A_3, A_5\}{A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč},{A4‚Äč,A5‚Äč},{A3‚Äč,A5‚Äč}.
For all pairs in the subsequence {A3,A4,A5}\{A_3, A_4, A_5\}{A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč,A5‚Äč}, (lcm(3,Si)‚ąíSi)=(lcm(3,Sj)‚ąíSj)=0(lcm(3, S_i) -S_i) = (lcm(3, S_j) - S_j) = 0(lcm(3,Si‚Äč)‚ąíSi‚Äč)=(lcm(3,Sj‚Äč)‚ąíSj‚Äč)=0.
